events.label: assistant
events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Bottom line: the staged breaker/conductor pairing violates NEC 240.4 — the OCPD's ampere rating exceeds the actual protected ampacity of the conductor once 110.14(C) terminal-temperature limits are applied (likely because 90°C insulation ampacity was used instead of the 60°C/75°C terminal rating, or the 240.4(D) small-conductor cap was ignored). Marked `fail`/`risk: high` since energizing as-staged would leave the conductor unprotected against overload; correction requires either downsizing the breaker to match the corrected conductor ampacity or upsizing the conductor to match the load-driven breaker size, verified against the engineered load schedule.
